2605.19782.title: Prior Knowledge or Search? A Study of LLM Agents in Hardware-Aware Code Optimization
2605.19782.paper_summary: LLM discovery and optimization systems are increasingly applied across domains, implementing a common propose-evaluate-revise loop. Such optimization or discovery progresses via context conditioning on received feedback from an environment. However, as modern LLM agents are increasingly complex in their structure, it is difficult to evaluate which components contribute the most, and when and how this exploration may fail. We answer these questions through three controlled experiments. Our findings: (1) In pure black-box optimization, LLMs act as greedy optimizers. (2) In zero-shot kernel generation, providing explicit input-size information has no measurable effect, models converge to the same kernel parameters regardless of size or temperature, as though the size instruction were invisible. Moreover, when tasked to perform kernel optimization for uncommon kernel sizes, performance sharply degrades regardless of the language used. (3) In feedback-loop kernel optimization, CUDA improves monotonically under iterative feedback, while TVM IR actively degrades, which demonstrates that kernel optimization degrades when models operate with low-density language. Our results conclude that LLMs in code optimization tasks highly depend on pretrained priors rather than provided feedback or agentic structure.
2605.19782.paper_summary_zh: LLM 發現和優化系統越來越多地應用於各個領域，實施一個共同的提出-評估-修訂循環。這種優化或發現通過對環境中接收到的反饋進行上下文調節來進行。然而，隨著現代 LLM 代理在其結構上變得越來越複雜，評估哪些組件貢獻最大，以及這種探索何時以及如何可能失敗變得困難。我們通過三個受控實驗來回答這些問題。我們的發現：（1）在純黑箱優化中，LLM 作為貪婪優化器運作。（2）在零樣本內核生成中，提供明確的輸入大小信息沒有可測量的影響，模型無論大小或溫度如何都會收斂到相同的內核參數，彷彿大小指令是不可見的。此外，當被要求對不常見的內核大小進行內核優化時，無論使用何種語言，性能都會急劇下降。（3）在反饋循環內核優化中，CUDA 在迭代反饋下單調改善，而 TVM IR 積極退化，這表明當模型在低密度語言下運作時，內核優化會退化。我們的結果總結出 LLM 在代碼優化任務中高度依賴預訓練的先驗知識，而不是提供的反饋或代理結構。
2605.19782.author: Dmitry Redko et.al.
2605.19782.authors: Dmitry Redko, Albert Fazlyev, Konstantin Sozykin, Maria Ivanova, Evgeny Burnaev, Egor Shvetsov
